content:
{
  "title": "Dependency-Aware Prompting: Leveraging Static Analysis Trees to Guide LLM Code Generation",
  "problem": "Existing prompting methods for code generation often overlook explicit representation of code dependencies and structure, leading to errors in logic, variable usage, and function integration. We address the lack of structured, dependency-aware context in LLM prompts for complex code synthesis.",
  "motivation": "Current LLMs struggle with generating correct code for tasks involving intricate interdependencies or multi-function modules. Static analysis tools can extract detailed dependency graphs from code specifications or partial programs, but this information is not directly incorporated into prompts. Integrating this explicit structure can improve logical coherence and reduce code errors.",
  "method": "We propose Dependency-Aware Prompting (DAP), which augments natural language prompts with automatically generated static analysis trees (e.g., abstract syntax trees or call graphs) derived from task descriptions or partial code. The prompt includes both the problem statement and a machine-readable serialization of dependencies (e.g., JSON-formatted AST or call graph). The LLM is instructed to use this structure to guide code synthesis, ensuring that generated code respects the given dependencies. DAP can be applied both in zero-shot and few-shot settings, with the dependency information serving as an explicit blueprint for code structure.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate DAP on HumanEval, MBPP, and custom datasets featuring multi-function and dependency-rich tasks. Baselines will include standard prompting, chain-of-thought, and structured CoT methods. Metrics will cover functional correctness, structural adherence (matching dependencies), and code quality (e.g., compilation, unit-test pass rate)."
}
